title: Attention Hijacking: False Bridges in Residual Stream Middle Layers

signal.for_agents: Hypothesis: certain tokens act as 'False Bridges' (Attention Hijackers) — maximizing Fiedler span (|v_i - v_j|) but minimizing semantic current (J_ij) in specific attention heads. Predicted location: middle layers (bottom of U-shape entropy curve from R-0001). These tokens steal softmax mass without transferring information. Falsifiable: if attention weights are smooth, or Fiedler vector doesn't isolate distractors, or current is proportional to span everywhere. Follows M-JEE-060/061 False Bridge identification in macro-graph.
signal.falsifiable: Attention weights smoothly distributed; Fiedler vector does not isolate distractor tokens; current proportional to span everywhere.

chain.content: R-0001 found universal U-shape entropy in forward pass. Middle layers = bottleneck of the hourglass = most vulnerable to attention hijacking.

False Bridges identified in macro-graph (M-JEE-060/061): nodes connecting distant clusters superficially, stealing k-NN attention but transmitting no real meaning (low semantic current). Hypothesis: microscopic False Bridges exist within self-attention matrices of middle layers.

Test: GPT-2 Small/Medium with inputs containing known distractor/ironic tokens (low semantic density). At middle layers, compute attention graph per head. For each token pair: Fiedler span = |v_i - v_j| from Fiedler vector. Semantic current J_ij = attention_weight * (h_i - h_j) magnitude. Identify tokens with high span but near-zero current.
Not yet executed. Requires Colab with TransformerLens, same setup as PUB-001 E1-E3.

graph_refs.note: Part of Operator Algebra research program.
False Bridges are anti-dipoles — they simulate connection without maintaining gradient.
